description: Closes the two remaining open phases of ADR-2026-04-13-2300 (Brain Daemon — Supervisor Loop): P4 finishes the .hex/daemon.toml schema with per-check toggles + interval; P5 wires brain_tick events into the Solid.js dashboard so operators get a live supervisor-health view. Execution: P4.1 edits hex-cli/src/commands/sched.rs which is on the SafeFileWriter protected-files list, so this workplan does NOT auto-enqueue under `hex brain enqueue workplan` — it requires a human-supervised run (interactive coder agent or direct edit). P5.1 only touches dashboard assets and could be promoted to a standalone enqueueable workplan if P5 wants to ship ahead of P4.
feature: Brain Daemon — Config Toggles + Dashboard Stream (ADR P4 + P5)

phases.description: Extend .hex/daemon.toml beyond [notify] to cover the [daemon] interval/backoff knobs and the [checks] per-check toggles described in ADR-2026-04-13-2300 §3. Daemon reads config at startup; missing file → defaults (everything on, 60s interval); unknown keys ignored.
phases.name: Per-check toggles + interval in .hex/daemon.toml

tasks.description: In hex-cli/src/commands/sched.rs: extend DaemonTomlFile with optional [daemon] (interval_secs, max_consecutive_failures, backoff_on_failure) and [checks] sections (binary_freshness, workplan_reconcile, worktree_cleanup, architecture_grade — all bool, default true). Add a load_daemon_config() helper alongside load_notify_config(). The daemon tick reads check toggles before running each step and skips disabled ones with tracing::debug!. CLI flags --interval and --max-failures continue to win over the file. Non-breaking: missing sections → defaults.
tasks.name: Extend DaemonTomlFile schema + per-check gating

phases.description: Add a brain_tick consumer to the Solid.js dashboard so operators see live supervisor health. Polls/streams the existing /api/events endpoint filtered by event_type=brain_tick, displays last tick timestamp, last duration_ms, recent issues count, auto-fixes applied, and a sparkline of tick cadence.
phases.name: Dashboard live brain_tick stream

tasks.description: Add a Solid.js view component under hex-nexus/assets/src/components/views/ (e.g. BrainSupervisor.tsx) that calls GET /api/events?event_type=brain_tick on a 2s poll (mirrors the existing pattern in `hex brain watch`). Render: last tick (relative time), tick interval, last duration_ms, issues_found, auto_fixed counts, and a 20-tick rolling sparkline. Wire into the dashboard route table. Rebuild hex-nexus/release binary so rust-embed picks up the new asset.
tasks.name: BrainSupervisor dashboard view
